meta_title: For Students: Antigravity project template to Solve stuck with basic apps (Advanced Checklist)
meta_description: Learn how to Antigravity project template and overcome stuck with basic apps. A Advanced-level Checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code.
h2_structure: The Problem: stuck with basic apps

content.seo_title: For Students: Antigravity Project Template for AI Apps
content.seo_description: Break through the tutorial trap. Use the Antigravity project template and Claude Code to master advanced AI development and build your first production app.
content.answer_block: Stop building 'to-do' lists. The Antigravity project template provides a production-grade skeleton, integrating real-time state management and AI orchestration. By coupling this with Claude Code, you automate boilerplate, enforce architectural patterns, and transition from writing simple scripts to engineering complex, scalable AI-native applications that command employer attention.
content.intro:
You’ve finished the tutorials, but your portfolio is a graveyard of abandoned 'hello world' apps. The gap between following a video and architecting a real-world system feels like a chasm, leaving you stuck in a cycle of surface-level learning. You aren't failing; you're just using the wrong scaffolding.

This isn't another generic tutorial. We are deploying the Antigravity project template—a high-fidelity framework designed for production AI engineering. By combining this architecture with the iterative power of Claude Code, you will stop mimicking code and start engineering solutions that solve actual, high-value problems.

steps.title: Initialize the Antigravity Scaffold
steps.description: Establish a production-ready directory structure that separates logic, state, and UI components from day one.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Initialize a new Antigravity project with the 'Advanced-SaaS' template. Include TypeScript, modular state management, and pre-configured API routing.

steps.title: Define the AI Orchestration Layer
steps.description: Map out your AI agent's logic, ensuring state is persisted between user interactions.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a service layer for a multi-step AI agent that handles user context and maintains persistent conversation history using the Antigravity state provider.

steps.title: Implement Type-Safe API Routes
steps.description: Ensure your frontend and backend communicate via strictly typed interfaces to prevent runtime crashes.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create an API route handler that validates incoming request bodies using Zod and interacts with the previously defined agent service.

steps.title: Automate Integration Testing
steps.description: Use Claude Code to generate tests that verify your AI responses match your expected schema.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a suite of integration tests for the /api/analyze route, mocking the AI response to ensure schema compliance.

steps.title: Deploy to Production Edge
steps.description: Configure your project for low-latency deployment, ensuring your AI app is accessible globally.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Configure the deployment manifest for Vercel/Edge functions, optimizing for cold-start performance.

pro_tips: Always define your Zod schemas before writing the business logic; it acts as the contract for your entire application.
Use Claude Code's '--watch' mode to catch architectural drift as you add new features to your project template.
Keep your AI prompt engineering separate from your code; use a JSON config file that Antigravity can load dynamically.
Instrument your code with OpenTelemetry early; you need observability to debug AI hallucinations in production.
Treat your project documentation as code—use Claude to generate README updates whenever you modify the core API structure.
content.next_steps: Once the foundation is live, implement a user feedback loop (thumbs up/down) and connect it to a database to fine-tune your model performance over time.
content.conversion_block: You have the architecture and the tools to stop being a student and start being an engineer. The industry is waiting for developers who can ship, not just study. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
